'''Keith Mason''' (born {{birth date|1982|1|20|df=y}}) is a former [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s.

He played as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]],  [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]],  [[Castleford Tigers]] (two spells) and [[Huddersfield Giants]], as well as [[Norths Devils|Brisbane Norths]], and for [[Melbourne Storm]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL). Mason played in three Challenge Cup finals, winning one in 2004 for St Helens against Wigan in Cardiff. He also won the [[League Leaders' Shield]] with St Helens in 2005.

He won two caps for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at international level, and also played for Great Britain and England Under-21s.

==Background==
Mason was born [[Dewsbury]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

His son [[Lukas Mason]] is a professional rugby league footballer who plays for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>[https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/story-telling/12196/13049699/keith-mason-how-former-super-league-star-and-walking-contradiction-did-it-against-all-odds From Super League to the silver screen: How 'walking contradiction' Mason did it against all odds]</ref>

==Club career==
In a 14-year career Mason played for four Super League teams; the Wakefield Trinity Wildcats, St Helens, the Castleford Tigers, and the Huddersfield Giants, as well as playing in the NRL for two seasons with Melbourne Storm.

In 2009 Mason won the 'Coaches' Player of The Year', the 'Directors' Player of The Year', and the Huddersfield Giants 'Man of Steel Award',{{citation needed|date=May 2018}} he also appeared in a Challenge Cup final in the same year at the Wembley Stadium against Warrington Wolves, a boyhood dream come true for Mason, also appearing in 2 more Challenge Cup finals in 2004 which he won for St Helens against Wigan at the Millennium Stadium playing {{rlp|pr}}.

==International honours==
Keith Mason won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] while at Wakefield Trinity Wildcats in 2001 against England, and while at Melbourne Storm in 2002 against New Zealand in 2002.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He also represented England at under-21 level on the tour to South Africa in 2001.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/wales/1858327.stm |title=Mason frustrated by injury |publisher=BBC News |date=6 March 2002 |access-date=29 June 2017}}</ref>

==Acting career==
At the end of the 2013 season, Mason retired from playing rugby league in order to pursue a career as an actor. He appeared in the film ''[[Skin Traffik]]'' alongside [[Mickey Rourke]] and [[Daryl Hannah]], and has subsequently appeared in the TV series ''[[Peaky Blinders (TV series)|Peaky Blinders]]''.<ref>{{cite web|title=Castleford Tigers player is Hollywood star|url=http://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/news/latest-news/top-stories/castleford-tigers-player-is-hollywood-star-1-6345824|website=Yorkshire Evening Post|accessdate=18 January 2015|date=1 January 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/keith-mason-footy-film-and-fighting|title=Keith Mason: Footy, film and fighting |work=Love Rugby League |date=6 May 2016 |accessdate=29 June 2017}}</ref>

In 2021 he starred in, and produced, his first feature film, ''Imperative.''<ref>{{cite web |last1=Pattison |first1=Cam |title=Ex-Super League player Keith Mason's own movie Imperative could be headed to Netflix, Amazon and other video platforms |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/ex-super-league-player-keith-masons-own-movie-imperative-could-be-headed-to-netflix-amazon-and-other-video-platforms-66189/ |website=Serious About Rugby League |access-date=29 January 2023 |date=15 January 2023}}</ref>

==Political career==
Mason is a member of the [[Workers Party of Britain]], and stood for the party in the [[Wakefield and Rothwell]] constituency at the [[2024 United Kingdom general election|2024 general election]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 May 2024 |title=Ex-St Helens, Huddersfield Giants and Wigan Warriors prop to stand as candidate in General Election |website=Total RL |url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-st-helens-huddersfield-giants-and-wigan-warriors-prop-to-stand-as-candidate-in-general-election/}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=STATEMENT OF PERSONS NOMINATED AND NOTICE OF POLL |url=https://www.wakefield.gov.uk/media/a1nabemk/wf-ukpge-sopn-notice-of-poll-wakefield-rothwell.docx |website=[[Wakefield Council]] |access-date=11 June 2024}}</ref>  He had been disowned by the party during the campaign, but was subsequently reinstated.<ref>{{cite web|website=5Pillars|date=13 Jun 2024|title=Keith Mason reinstated as Workers Party candidate after apology to Muslims|url=https://5pillarsuk.com/2024/06/13/keith-mason-reinstated-as-workers-party-candidate-after-apology-to-muslims/}}</ref>
